goal: Implement phase 2 of the repository's agent context-engineering architecture using the existing single-entry orchestrator.
constraints: Keep the rollout incremental and compatible with the existing single-entry orchestrator.
Do not pretend editor-managed memory notes are ordinary repo files for validation purposes.
Use the existing run-artifact and context-packet contracts instead of adding a separate memory artifact.

completed_steps.summary: Classified the work as a brownfield improvement and mapped the delta from phase 1.
completed_steps.output: Identified three missing operational behaviors: run-artifact activation, repo-memory hydration recording, and a concrete brownfield trace.
completed_steps.verification: Read the active phase-1 spec, workflow manifest, packet schema, examples, and validator wiring.

completed_steps.summary: Checked whether repo memory notes were exposed as normal workspace files.
completed_steps.output: Confirmed that `/memories/repo` is editor-managed here and should be modeled by source metadata instead of filesystem validation.
completed_steps.verification: A direct directory listing for `/memories/repo` failed with ENOENT in the local workspace.

current_step.title: Upgrade the packet and activate run artifacts for real multi-step brownfield work
current_step.input_contract: Current agent-platform metadata, phase-1 spec, and existing packet plus run-artifact examples.
current_step.output_contract: A richer packet contract, active run-artifact policy, a concrete brownfield example pair, and passing validation.
current_step.done_condition: The validator accepts the richer packet and the new brownfield examples, and the default workflow docs describe the same behavior.

task_graph.title: Upgrade the packet and activate run artifacts
task_graph.input_contract: Workflow manifest, packet schema, examples, and validator.
task_graph.done_condition: Schema, examples, and validation agree on the richer contract.

task_graph.title: Update orchestrator, prompts, and docs to use the richer packet
task_graph.input_contract: Default orchestrator, prompt wrappers, and contributor docs.
task_graph.output_contract: Active run-artifact and memory-hydration guidance in the default path.
task_graph.done_condition: The default brownfield and bugfix prompts describe the same behavior as the manifest and packet.

task_graph.title: Validate the packet and brownfield example pair
task_graph.input_contract: Updated metadata, examples, validator script, and docs.
task_graph.output_contract: A passing agent-platform validation run and final traceability notes.

working_memory.focus: Phase-2 contract activation for run artifacts and repo-memory hydration.
scratchpad: Upgrade packet schema to record active run-artifact state.
Add loaded repo-memory sources as summaries instead of raw note dumps.
Add a concrete brownfield packet and run-artifact example pair.
working_memory.stale_data_policy: Keep only the active phase-2 subtask live and compress earlier tool output into completed-step summaries.

episodic_memory.episode_summary: Phase 1 is complete. Phase 2 is making run artifacts and repo-memory hydration operational in the default path.
retained_facts: The default orchestrator already uses the packet contract, but it does not yet activate run artifacts by default.
Repo-memory notes are editor-managed in this environment and should be referenced by source metadata, not filesystem validation.
The sync validator already checks packet and run-artifact examples and can be extended for the new brownfield pair.

semantic_memory.retrieval_policy: Load repo or editor-managed memory only when the current brownfield subtask needs missing facts, then keep a short source-backed summary in the packet.

loaded_memories.summary: Use existing agent customization primitives and keep the workflow inside the repo's orchestrator architecture.
The repository's canonical path is a single-entry orchestrator with metadata under `.github/agent-platform/`.

deferred_queries: session-specific packet examples if the validator reveals a missing field

run_artifact.task_ledger_policy: Record a taskLedger entry after each completed subtask because this workflow spans specs, metadata, prompts, docs, and validation.

context_health.trim_strategy: Keep the goal anchor, classification, current step, loaded memory summaries, and latest evidence; drop stale raw output after each finished subtask.
context_health.stale_output_policy: Raw output expires after the subtask that used it unless it is promoted into evidence or episodic memory.
context_health.last_action: Compressed the memory-probe result into completed_steps instead of carrying the failed directory listing forward.

self_check: Before responding, verify the phase-2 output activates run artifacts, records hydrated memory sources, and validates the concrete brownfield example pair.
